title: 10 best herbal teas for gum pain

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm tea's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the jaw and gums, reducing tension and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders and gum inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in lemon balm tea, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums and surrounding tissues, alleviating pain and discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm tea's antimicrobial properties can help to combat the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause gum infections and inflammation, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing the risk of dental problems.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, teas helps with gum pain because it contains rosmarinic acid, a potent anti-inflammatory compound. This compound works to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the gums, providing relief from pain and inflammation. Additionally, lemon balm teas have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to relax tense jaw muscles and reduce tension headaches often associated with gum pain. This natural remedy provides a soothing and gentle solution for gum-related discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic Acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.
Luteolin: A flavonoid that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe gum pain and promote healing by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.
Volatile Oils (Limonene and Pinene): Terpenes that have local anesthetic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to numb the gum area and reduce pain and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe gum pain.
Stems: The stems of Melissa officinalis are also used to make teas, as they contain similar properties to the leaves and can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums.
Roots: The roots of the plant are used to add depth and complexity to the tea, and their potential anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate gum pain and promote oral health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves when they are in full bloom and the flowers are in perfect condition.
Dry the freshly harvested melissa officinalis leaves in a warm place with good air circulation for 7 to 10 days.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried melissa officinalis leaves with 1 cup of boiling water to make a soothing tea.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes and then strain the tea to remove the dried herbs.
Drink the melissa officinalis tea immediately after straining to reap its health benefits and calming effects.

remedy_properties: Menthol content: Menthol in peppermint tea helps numb the gums, reducing pain and inflammation, making it an effective natural analgesic.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which help reduce swelling and alleviate gum pain caused by conditions like gingivitis or periodontitis.
Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of peppermint tea can help combat bacterial infections that cause gum pain, such as dental caries, plaque, or abscesses.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, teas helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol in peppermint tea has a numbing effect on the gums, providing temporary relief from pain and discomfort. Additionally, peppermint tea's antibacterial properties help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections that can cause gum pain. Its soothing and refreshing properties also promote good oral health, making it a popular remedy for gum-related issues. Regular consumption may also reduce gum inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol acts as a local anesthetic, numbing the affected area and providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with gum inflammation.
Eugenol: Eugenol has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the gums.
Caryophyllene: Caryophyllene is a sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make teas for gum pain due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Utilized in teas to alleviate gum pain due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Also used to make teas for gum pain due to their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves and stems from the plant in the morning for optimal flavor and aroma.
Rinse the harvested plant material under cool running water to remove dirt and debris quickly.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area for infusion immediately.
Steep one teaspoon of chopped plant material in one cup of boiling water for five minutes carefully.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids before serving immediately.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas Coneflower tea contains compounds like triterpenoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, providing relief from gum pain and discomfort.

Antibacterial properties: The tea's antimicrobial agents, such as rosmarinic acid, help combat bacteria that can cause periodontal disease and gum infections, reducing the risk of tooth loss and promoting healing.

Analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties: The bioactive compounds in Kansas Coneflower tea, including sesquiterpenes, have been shown to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce pain and discomfort associated with gum disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, teas helps with gum pain because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated gum tissues. The tea's active compounds, such as alkylamides and polyphenols, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with gum issues like gingivitis and periodontitis. Additionally, Echinacea angustifolia tea has antimicrobial properties that help combat gum infections, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing discomfort. This natural remedy offers a promising solution for those seeking relief from gum pain.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation.
Alkaloids (echinatin, echinacoside, and caffic acid): These alkaloids exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to soothe gum pain and discomfort.
Phenolic acids (caffeic acid and ferulic acid): These compounds possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat gum inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia contain a high concentration of alkylamides, which are known to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate gum pain.
Leaves: The leaves are used in teas for gum pain because they contain compounds that have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections causing the pain.
Roots: The roots also contain echinacoside, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, making it useful for gum pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 ounces of dried echinacea angustifolia roots from a reputable supplier or your garden in autumn.
Gently clean the dried roots with a soft brush to remove any dirt or debris from storage.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the cleaned roots with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the bioactive compounds into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard the solids before serving.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum disease or injuries, making it a natural remedy for soothing gum discomfort.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice tea have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the gums from further damage and inflammation, promoting overall oral health and reducing gum pain.

Mucilage: The mucilage in licorice root tea forms a protective barrier that soothes and calms irritated gum tissues, providing relief from discomfort, inflammation, and bleeding associated with gum disease or injuries.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, teas helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. Additionally, licorice tea's antibacterial properties help combat infections that can cause gum pain, promoting overall oral health and a reduction in discomfort. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 tablespoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier or local herbalist.
Rinse the dried roots under cold running water for 10 seconds to remove impurities.
Boil 1 liter of water in a teapot or kettle for 5 minutes to achieve optimal temperature.
Steep the dried roots in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release their active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine mesh sieve into a cup and discard the solids immediately.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Many herbal teas, such as peppermint, chamomile, and ginger, contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the gums, thereby relieving gum pain.

Antioxidants: Herbal teas like green tea, white tea, and hibiscus are rich in antioxidants that can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the mouth, which can contribute to gum pain and other oral health issues.

Astringent properties: Herbs like sage, myrrh, and licorice root have astringent properties that can help reduce bleeding, shrink swollen tissues, and soothe irritated gums, providing relief from gum pain and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, teas helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The catechins present in Camellia sinensis teas have been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gums. The antioxidants in tea also help to combat free radicals that can cause inflammation and irritation in the gums. Stems: Used to make tea, as it contains flavonoids and polyphenols that help reduce inflammation and ease gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Select high-quality camellia sinensis leaves with proper oxidation and drying to ensure optimal flavor and aroma.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried camellia sinensis leaves per 8 oz of water for a standard serving.
Heat water to a temperature of 200-212 degrees fahrenheit for the perfect infusion time.
Steep the leaves for 3-5 minutes to allow the flavors and antioxidants to be fully released.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves to enjoy the final product.

remedy_recipe: Harvest stevia rebaudiana leaves and flowers when they are in full bloom and the plant is in its prime stage.

Combine 1 teaspoon of dried stevia leaves and 1 cup of boiling water to make a strong tea infusion.
Steep the tea mixture for 5 to 7 minutes to allow the flavors and properties to fully release.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes with healthy roots for optimal flavor and aroma.

Combine 2-3 slices of the sliced rhizome with 1 quart of boiling water for steeping time.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors and oils to infuse the water.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing gum inflammation and reducing pain.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried vaccinium macrocarpon leaves and flowers into a heat-resistant cup.
Heat filtered water to a temperature of 100°c to 105°c in a kettle or on the stovetop.
Pour the heated water over the vaccinium macrocarpon ingredients in the cup, allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another heat-resistant cup, discarding solids.
Allow the tea to cool to drinking temperature and enjoy immediately, with a recommended serving size of one cup three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood teas helps with gum pain because it contains compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed as a tea, these compounds can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums, providing relief from conditions such as gingivitis and periodontitis. Additionally, wormwood's antibacterial properties may also help combat underlying infections that contribute to gum pain. As a result, drinking herbal wormwood teas can be an effective and natural way to alleviate gum pain and promote healthy gums.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood tea's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections in the mouth, reducing inflammation and pain associated with gum disease.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in wormwood tea reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums, alleviating pain and promoting healing.
Astringent properties: Wormwood tea's astringent properties help constrict blood vessels, reduce bleeding, and soothe irritated mucous membranes, providing relief from gum irritation and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wormwood roots are used to make teas for gum pain due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and ease discomfort.
Barks: The barks of the wormwood plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antibacterial properties that can help combat gum infections and soothe sore gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried artemisia absinthium leaves or 4 teaspoons of fresh leaves for every 8 oz of water you plan to use.
Boil 8 oz of pure water in a pot and reduce the heat to medium-low once it reaches a rolling boil.
Add the harvested artemisia absinthium leaves to the pot and steep for 5-7 minutes or according to the desired strength.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and discard the leaves.
Let the tea cool down slightly before drinking it, as it is best consumed warm but not hot.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively soothe irritated gums and reduce swelling. Additionally, dandelion tea's antimicrobial properties work to eliminate harmful microorganisms that can cause gum disease, promoting a healthy oral environment and alleviating discomfort associated with gum pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and triterpenoids, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, alleviating pain and discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents present in dandelion tea, like flavonoids and phenolic acids, inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections and pain, promoting healthy gum tissue.
Antioxidant properties: Dandelion tea's high antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissues, reducing oxidative stress and promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bitter compounds that help reduce inflammation and combat infection, making them effective against gum pain.
Fruits: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe gum pain and prevent further irritation.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried taraxacum officinale root per cup.
Bring the measured amount of water to a boil in a pot on high heat.
Add the dried taraxacum officinale root to the boiling water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking it, consuming within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle teas helps with gum pain because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the gums. The tea's antioxidant-rich profile also helps to combat oxidative stress, a common underlying factor of gum inflammation. Additionally, stinging nettle has been shown to stimulate blood flow, promoting healthy oral circulation and soothing irritated gum tissue, thereby providing relief from persistent gum pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle tea has anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, which reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, alleviating pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in stinging nettle tea, such as polyphenols, help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall gum health.

Antimicrobial properties: Stinging nettle tea has antimicrobial compounds like caffeic acid, which inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections and pain, reducing the risk of complications like periodontitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Barks: Contains salicin, a natural analgesic similar to aspirin, which helps relieve gum pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems, or use dried herb from a trusted supplier.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried herb or 2-3 teaspoons of fresh herb with boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and leaving only the clear liquid.
Drink the tea immediately, allowing the anti-inflammatory properties of urtica dioica to help soothe gum pain within 15-30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the gums. The tea's antiseptic compounds also work to combat bacterial infections that can cause gum pain and inflammation, promoting a healthy mouth environment. Additionally, yarrow's natural analgesic properties help to numb the affected area, providing relief from sharp pains and sensitivity. By drinking yarrow teas regularly, individuals can alleviate gum pain and maintain a healthy, comfortable smile.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums, making it an effective remedy for gum inflammation and discomfort.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of yarrow tea help combat bacterial infections in the mouth, including those that can cause gum disease and pain, promoting a healthy oral environment.

Styptic: Yarrow's styptic properties, which help stop bleeding and reduce swelling, make it an effective remedy for treating minor cuts and abrasions in the mouth, including those caused by gum recession or tooth extraction.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain in gums.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which have antibacterial properties, helping to combat bacterial infections that cause gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in amounts of 2-3 teaspoons.
Combine the harvested herbs with 8 ounces of boiling water and let steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding solids.
Add honey to taste if desired, as some people find it sweetens and soothes the gum pain.
Drink the warm tea immediately, as its anti-inflammatory properties will work best within 20-30 minutes of consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The tea's flavonoids, specifically quercetin, also possess potent antioxidant abilities that combat free radicals responsible for gum inflammation. remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Fennel tea's high antioxidant content helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, which can alleviate gum pain and prevent further damage.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The polyphenols present in fennel tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums.
Anesthetic and analgesic properties: Fennel tea contains compounds like anethole, which has been shown to possess local anesthetic and analgesic effects, helping to numb and relieve gum pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have a calming effect on gum inflammation and can help reduce pain and discomfort.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe gums and alleviate pain associated with gum disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried foeniculum vulgare seeds from your local market or online supplier weighing approximately one tablespoon.
Boil filtered water in a teapot or kettle for exactly five minutes.
Add the one-tablespoon dried foeniculum vulgare seeds to the boiling water allowing them to steep for ten minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a large cup discarding the solids and retaining the flavorful liquid.
Allow the tea to cool for five minutes before consumption.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum pain that are treated with herbal teas are conditions such as toothaches, mouth sores, and swollen gums. Herbs like peppermint, chamomile, and eucalyptus have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate discomfort and reduce swelling in the mouth and gums. These teas can also soothe sore throats, relax tense muscles, and promote overall oral health when consumed regularly as part of a healthy routine.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for gum pain are a natural remedy that provides relief from discomfort and inflammation in the gums. These teas contain anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and calm irritated gums. Examples of herbal teas that help with gum pain include peppermint, eucalyptus, and chamomile. Drinking these teas can improve lives by reducing pain and swelling, promoting relaxation, and enhancing oral health, making daily activities more manageable and enjoyable.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with gum pain is a blend of peppermint, chamomile, and eucalyptus. Peppermint tea's antibacterial properties help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria, while chamomile tea's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the gums. Eucalyptus tea adds its decongestant properties to help ease gum swelling and promote healing. Drinking a combination of these teas can help alleviate gum pain, reduce inflammation, and promote overall oral health. This blend can be consumed hot or cold, depending on individual preference.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of pharmacy & bioallied sciences, a comprehensive electronic database search was conducted in PubMed, CINAHL, MEDLINE, EMBASE, Google, Google Scholar, and SCOPUS to find natural solutions for oral health care. The study found that medicinal plants such as Aloe Vera, which has antifungal and soothing properties, have excellent applications in treating gum disorders and preventing tooth decay. As a result, herbal teas made from these plants can be effective in reducing gum pain by stimulating the body's healing process and relieving inflammation.
